obligations.reason: The scope exposes SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM instances and muxes jtag_req_valid or dmi_req_valid into debug_req_valid; visible assignments show selection by jtag_enable but not an authorization condition. Need check whether this is only testbench behavior or a reachable debug subject to protected state, and whether mediation exists at the request source or sink.
obligations.subject: JTAG or DMI requester via SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM
obligations.operation: issue debug/DMI read or write and receive response
obligations.object: debug module/DMI registers and any memory/system state reachable through Debug address region
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or explicit enable/lifecycle gating in addition to jtag_enable selection before debug requests can affect protected debug module state or system memory
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness JTAG/DTM debug mux -> debug_req/debug_resp -> SoC debug/DMI path
required_evidence_slots: protected object or debug address region
source locations for mux and sink
knowledge_used: evidence closure requires subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows harness wiring but not downstream debug module internals or jtag_enable declaration/source.

obligations.reason: connectivity_mapping computes connectivity_map_o from access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] but has a special-case alias where target j==6 also permits access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]. Check whether this intentionally aliases PLIC and CLINT or grants access to one protected interrupt-controller region under the other's permission.
obligations.subject: AXI manager selected by index i and current priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: route AXI read/write request from manager to subordinate
obligations.object: protected subordinate address regions including AES, ROM2/ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM
obligations.expected_guard: connectivity for each manager/subordinate must be gated by the correct privilege-indexed access_ctrl_i entry, with no unintended alias between protected targets
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap access_ctrl_i/priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_map_o -> AXI routing to subordinate ports
state_conditions_to_check: all privilege levels indexed by priv_lvl_i
obligations.uncertainty: The visible fact layer gives enum indices and mapping expression but not full access_ctrl initialization/update policy or final router response behavior.

obligations.reason: The wrapper forwards start/end addresses and sets s_valid_rule to all ones. Check whether invalid/unused rules or overlapping address ranges can route to protected regions without an explicit bounds/validity guard, especially with address bases listed in ariane_soc_pkg.
obligations.subject: AXI master issuing AW/AR transactions
obligations.operation: decode and route read/write address to subordinate region
obligations.object: AXI subordinate address map and protected memory/MMIO regions
obligations.expected_guard: address-range/rule validity should exclude unmapped or protected ranges unless corresponding access control and privilege checks permit the target
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap start_addr_i/end_addr_i and s_valid_rule='1 -> AXI node address decode/rules
obligations.uncertainty: Full parameter values/end addresses and axi_node internal behavior are not present in the facts.

obligations.reason: rom2 names an internal secure_reg and resets it from mem, but the always block preview shows writes to secure_reg[addr] when req_i and we_i are asserted. Check if the surrounding bus wrapper prevents unauthorized writes, or if ROM2 is writable without privilege/lock/lifecycle mediation.
obligations.subject: bus requester connected to rom2 port
obligations.operation: write secure_reg through req_i && we_i, and read rdata_o through req_i && !we_i
obligations.object: rom2 secure_reg memory initialized from mem
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 protected contents should be read-only or writes should be mediated by privilege/access-control/lifecycle guard; reads should be mediated if contents are secret/protected
state_conditions_to_check: addr_i index in and out of range
read after write visibility on rdata_o
required_evidence_slots: observed guard in rom2 or wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: Connection of rom2 instance to top-level bus and intended protection status of contents need confirmation.

obligations.reason: Read data is bounded by raddr_q < RomSize, but the write preview shows direct secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i with no visible in-module bounds check. Check whether index width makes out-of-range impossible or whether bus address filtering is relied on for permission and safety.
obligations.subject: requester driving rom2 addr_i/we_i
obligations.object: secure_reg memory array and adjacent state/raddr_q
obligations.expected_guard: writes to ROM2 memory should be bounded to RomSize or rejected/ignored for out-of-range addresses, with no corruption of adjacent state
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 addr_i index -> secure_reg write without visible bounds check
state_conditions_to_check: sim/synthesis handling of out-of-range array index
obligations.uncertainty: RomSize parameter and array declaration lines are not fully included in provided facts.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows access_ctrl[i][j] sliced from access_ctrl_reg and consumed by connectivity_mapping with priv_lvl_i. Because this matrix mediates AXI connectivity, check all write/update/reset paths for access_ctrl_reg and whether only authorized privilege/lifecycle states can loosen permissions.

obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or configurable access-control policy registers must only be writable by authorized privileged subject and have safe reset defaults
state_conditions_to_check: writes while lower privilege mode active
ability to clear/loosen restrictions after lock if any

obligations.reason: The access-control matrix indexes permissions by a single priv_lvl signal assigned from priv_lvl_processor. Check whether this privilege is associated with the actual AXI requester/transaction and cannot be stale or incorrect for debug/DMA/peripheral managers.

obligations.operation: select permission bit based on priv_lvl_i during routing
obligations.object: privilege-indexed access decision for all AXI routes

obligations.operation: enable test behavior or bypass normal mediation

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG requester through SimJTAG/dmi_jtag, or SimDTM DMI requester when jtag_enable[0] is false
evidence_slots.operation: Issue DMI/debug read or write requests via debug_req_valid/debug_req and receive debug_resp through debug_resp_valid/debug_resp_ready
evidence_slots.object: dm_top debug module DMI registers, Debug address region, and system/memory state reachable through dm_top AXI master/slave paths
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug authorization or explicit enable/lifecycle gating in addition to jtag_enable selection before debug requests can affect protected debug module state or system memory
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: debug_req_valid, debug_req, debug_resp_ready, jtag_resp_valid, and dmi_resp_valid are selected solely by jtag_enable[0]. dmi_req_valid is tied to zero only in the non-SimDTM branch. dm_top consumes the muxed debug_req_valid/debug_req directly. dmi_jtag has a jtag_key connection but no visible authorization decision is shown in the provided scope.
evidence_slots.path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:105 init_done=rst_ni; lines 112-117 debug request/response mux by jtag_enable[0]; lines 121,135,162 SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM instances; lines 185-213 dm_top DMI interface; lines 215-216 dm_top AXI connections
evidence_slots.state_condition: jtag_enable[0] selects JTAG vs DMI/SimDTM; reset/init_done follows rst_ni; debug_req_ready/debug_resp_valid are handshaken directly with dm_top; dmi_req_valid alternative is tied off only when SimDTM branch is excluded
evidence_slots.impact: Potential unauthenticated debug/DMI access if the debug requester is reachable in the target integration and neither dmi_jtag nor dm_top enforce authentication/lifecycle policy.

rtl_evidence.evidence: dmi_resp_valid gated only by inverse jtag_enable[0]
dm_top consumes debug_req_valid/debug_req and returns debug_resp
dm_top AXI master/slave connected to fabric

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dmi_jtag authentication behavior, dm_top authorization behavior, jtag_enable declaration/source beyond plusarg observation, and whether testharness debug interfaces are production reachable.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Visible harness muxes JTAG/SimDTM DMI requests into dm_top based only on jtag_enable[0], with no authorization guard in the inspected scope; however dmi_jtag/dm_top internals and testbench reachability prevent conclusive violation closure.
inspection_records.notes: Classified inconclusive rather than candidate violation because visible missing guard may be supplied by downstream debug modules or the path may be testbench-only.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI requester on subordinate/slave port index i selected by current priv_lvl_i
evidence_slots.operation: Route AXI read/write request from requester i to subordinate/manager target j
evidence_slots.object: Protected subordinate address regions, especially PLIC index 6 and CLINT index 7; also AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, and DRAM as indexed in ariane_soc_pkg
evidence_slots.expected_guard: connectivity for each manager/subordinate must be gated by the correct privilege-indexed access_ctrl_i entry, with no unintended alias between protected targets
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: For all i,j, connectivity_map_o[i][j] is assigned access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]). Since ariane_soc_pkg maps PLIC=6 and CLINT=7, a CLINT permission grants PLIC routing when j==6.
evidence_slots.path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:439-472 priv_lvl/access_ctrl passed to i_axi_xbar -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:389-400 connectivity_mapping -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 connectivity_map_o expression -> axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: For any requester i and privilege priv_lvl_i, if access_ctrl_i[i][6][priv_lvl_i]==0 and access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]==1, then connectivity_map_o[i][6]==1. Reset/default values are not required to demonstrate the alias condition, but determine exploitability at reset.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester authorized for CLINT but not PLIC can be routed to the protected PLIC interrupt-controller region under the CLINT permission bit.

rtl_evidence.evidence: s_connectivity_map is passed to axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Intentionality of the PLIC/CLINT alias and reset/update policy of access_ctrl_reg.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: connectivity_mapping aliases CLINT permission bit to PLIC connectivity: target j==6 (PLIC) is permitted when access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] (CLINT) is set, even if access_ctrl_i[i][6][priv_lvl_i] is clear.
inspection_records.notes: The alias effect is explicit in RTL and maps to distinct PLIC/CLINT indices in the address package.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI master issuing AW/AR transactions through axi_node_intf_wrap
evidence_slots.operation: Decode and route read/write address using configured start/end address rules
evidence_slots.object: AXI subordinate address map and protected memory/MMIO regions: Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM
evidence_slots.expected_guard: address-range/rule validity should exclude unmapped or protected ranges unless corresponding access control and privilege checks permit the target
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap assigns s_start_addr[0][i]=start_addr_i[i], s_end_addr[0][i]=end_addr_i[i], and s_valid_rule='1. Harness supplies start/end ranges from ariane_soc_pkg base and length constants. No dynamic invalid/unused-rule guard is visible in the wrapper.
evidence_slots.state_condition: s_valid_rule is always all ones during reset and normal operation; start/end values appear static; out-of-range and overlap handling depends on axi_node internals not included in the provided facts
evidence_slots.impact: Potential decode/routing exposure cannot be confirmed; no overlap was proven from listed package ranges, but unmatched-address response and validity semantics are unknown.

rtl_evidence.evidence: harness passes region bases and base+length-1 end addresses to i_axi_xbar

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: axi_node internal address decode, overlap priority, unmatched response behavior, and full untruncated harness end-address list confirmation.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: All address decode rules are marked valid with s_valid_rule='1, while rule start/end values are forwarded from the harness. Provided bases/lengths appear non-overlapping, but axi_node behavior for unmatched or invalid addresses is not visible.
inspection_records.notes: Not classified as violation because provided base/length values are plausibly non-overlapping and the decisive decode semantics are outside the visible scope.

evidence_slots.subject: bus requester connected to rom2 port via rom2_fuse/master[ariane_soc::ROM2] path
evidence_slots.operation: Write secure_reg through req_i && we_i; read rdata_o through req_i && !we_i with raddr_q capture
evidence_slots.object: rom2 secure_reg memory initialized from mem, including key/access-control constants
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM2 protected contents should be read-only or writes should be mediated by privilege/access-control/lifecycle guard; reads should be mediated if contents are secret/protected
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: On reset, secure_reg <= mem. After reset, if req_i is asserted and we_i is high, secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i. There is no guard other than req_i/we_i in rom2. rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q] when raddr_q < RomSize.
evidence_slots.path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:550 rom2_fuse connected to master[ariane_soc::ROM2] (observed by search) -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:5-13 rom2 port -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:32-44 write/read always_ff -> src/rom2/rom2.sv:47 rdata_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_ni low copies mem into secure_reg; after reset deassertion, req_i && we_i writes selected secure_reg index; req_i && !we_i captures raddr_q; later rdata_o exposes indexed secure_reg when raddr_q<RomSize
evidence_slots.impact: A requester that can reach ROM2 can overwrite key/access-control fuse mirror contents and read back secure state, undermining key integrity/confidentiality and policy configuration.

rtl_evidence.evidence: comment: ROM2 has all the keys
mem constants include access-control, JTAG, and AES key-like values

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact wrapper module around rom2_fuse and whether bus fabric access_ctrl prevents all unauthorized writes to ROM2.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: rom2 exposes a write path to secure_reg when req_i && we_i with no visible in-module privilege, lock, lifecycle, or read-only guard; comments identify ROM2 as containing keys.
inspection_records.notes: In-module absence of guard is sufficient for candidate violation because the protected object is explicitly key-like and writable.

evidence_slots.subject: requester driving rom2 addr_i/we_i
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes to ROM2 memory should be bounded to RomSize or rejected/ignored for out-of-range addresses, with no corruption of adjacent state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: RomSize is 4, secure_reg is declared [3:0][191:0], and the write index is addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]. Since $clog2(4)=2, the index is two bits addr_i[4:3], which can only select values 0..3, exactly matching secure_reg indices. The read path also guards raddr_q < RomSize.
evidence_slots.path: src/rom2/rom2.sv:13 secure_reg dimensions; line 15 RomSize=4; line 28 raddr_q width; lines 38 and 40 index expression; line 47 read bounds check
evidence_slots.state_condition: For all 64-bit addr_i values, addr_i[4:3] is a two-bit value in range 0..3. req_i && we_i writes only an in-range secure_reg entry. Non-power-of-two RomSize concern does not apply because RomSize is explicitly 4.
evidence_slots.impact: No out-of-range array write impact identified for the provided RTL parameters; this does not address the separate permission issue that in-range ROM2 writes are allowed.

inspection_records.notes: Satisfied narrowly for bounds safety because the concrete RomSize is a power of two and the index width matches the array size.

evidence_slots.subject: configuration logic or bus requester influencing access_ctrl_reg through ROM2/fuse path
evidence_slots.operation: configure/change access policy bits used for AXI routing
evidence_slots.expected_guard: software-visible or configurable access-control policy registers must only be writable by authorized privileged subject and have safe reset defaults
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: access_ctrl_reg is declared in the harness and sliced into access_ctrl[i][j] with no local guard. Search shows access_ctrl_reg is connected at tb/ariane_testharness.sv:555, consistent with ROM2/fuse logic. rom2 initializes secure_reg from mem but permits req_i && we_i writes to secure_reg. No reset/update/lock/privilege guard for access_ctrl_reg is present in the provided harness facts.
evidence_slots.path: src/rom2/rom2.sv secure_reg/mem access-control constants -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:555 .access_ctrl_reg(access_ctrl_reg) observed -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:450 access_ctrl slicing -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 connectivity_map_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset, ROM2 secure_reg can be modified by req_i && we_i; access_ctrl_reg values are used combinationally to derive access_ctrl and connectivity_map. Writes during active transfers and reset defaults are not fully visible.
evidence_slots.impact: If a bus requester can write the ROM2/fuse path or otherwise influence access_ctrl_reg, it can loosen fabric permissions for protected regions, bypassing intended isolation.

rtl_evidence.evidence: mem entries comment: access control master configuration bits
secure_reg indexed write under req_i && we_i

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The complete module instance around line 555, exact connection from secure_reg to access_ctrl_reg, and any external privilege/lock guard on writes to ROM2/fuse wrapper.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: access_ctrl_reg, which controls fabric permissions, is populated from ROM2/fuse path and sliced directly into access_ctrl without any visible software privilege/lock guard in the harness; ROM2 itself is writable, so policy can potentially be modified by bus-accessible writes.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is based on combined evidence: policy register directly controls routing and the apparent fuse/ROM2 source is writable without in-module mediation.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI requester whose route is mediated by priv_lvl_i, including CPU on slave[0] and debug module on slave[1]
evidence_slots.operation: select permission bit based on priv_lvl_i during routing
evidence_slots.object: privilege-indexed access decision for all AXI routes
evidence_slots.expected_guard: privilege level used for fabric mediation must be trustworthy, synchronized with the requesting master, and not shared incorrectly across concurrent/requesting subjects
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane outputs priv_lvl_processor, assigned to a single priv_lvl signal, which is passed to i_axi_xbar. connectivity_mapping uses this same priv_lvl_i to index access_ctrl_i for all requester indices i and targets j. The debug module has a separate AXI master connection on slave[1], but no separate debug/master privilege tag is visible.
evidence_slots.path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:600 ariane .priv_lvl_o(priv_lvl_processor) -> line 441 priv_lvl=priv_lvl_processor -> lines 471-472 i_axi_xbar priv_lvl_i/access_ctrl_i -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:430 access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i]
evidence_slots.state_condition: Privilege transitions during outstanding transactions and debug requests are not latched per transaction in the visible RTL. Non-CPU requesters share the current CPU priv_lvl value when connectivity_map is evaluated.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester may be granted or denied based on a privilege level belonging to a different subject or different time, enabling unauthorized routing for debug/DMA/peripheral masters when CPU privilege is high or causing inconsistent policy during privilege transitions.

rtl_evidence.evidence: CPU AXI master connected to slave[0]
debug module AXI master connected to slave[1]
access_ctrl_i indexed by shared priv_lvl_i for all i,j

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: axi_node timing of connectivity_map sampling, complete set of masters, and whether non-CPU masters are intentionally assigned CPU privilege by policy.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Fabric permission decisions for all requesters use a single priv_lvl value exported from the Ariane CPU, not a per-transaction/per-master privilege tag; debug module and other non-CPU or concurrent requesters can be mediated using stale or unrelated CPU privilege.
inspection_records.notes: Classified as candidate because subject/privilege binding is visibly global while multiple requester ports exist.

evidence_slots.subject: test interface or integration signal driving test_en_i/test_en
evidence_slots.operation: enable test behavior or bypass normal mediation
evidence_slots.object: AXI routing/access-control checks, debug module behavior, reset generation, and protected subordinate regions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: test-mode signals should be tied off or authenticated/lifecycle-gated in production and must not bypass access-control routing or expose protected data
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In ariane_testharness, test_en is assigned 1'b0 and connected to rstgen.test_mode_i, dm_top.testmode_i, and i_axi_xbar.test_en_i. axi_node_intf_wrap passes test_en_i through to axi_node.test_en_i. The provided wrapper facts do not show test_en_i altering connectivity_map or access_ctrl directly.
evidence_slots.path: tb/ariane_testharness.sv:74 test_en tie-off -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:94-99 rstgen -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:197 dm_top testmode_i -> tb/ariane_testharness.sv:468 i_axi_xbar test_en_i -> src/axi_node/src/axi_node_intf_wrap.sv:280 axi_node test_en_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: In this harness, test_en_i is 0 during reset and normal operation. In non-harness integration, test_en_i could be externally driven; simultaneous debug/JTAG behavior under test_en_i asserted is not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: No harness-local bypass is shown, but production integrations that can assert test_en_i may expose unverified test-mode behavior in axi_node or dm_top.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Internal test_en_i behavior in axi_node and dm_top, and production tie-off/lifecycle assumptions outside ariane_testharness.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Harness ties test_en to 1'b0 and passes it to rstgen, dm_top, and axi_node_intf_wrap/axi_node; no bypass is observed in the wrapper, but downstream uses inside axi_node/dm_top are not visible and other integrations could assert test_en_i.
inspection_records.notes: Harness tie-off satisfies local observation, but downstream and integration-level evidence is insufficient for closure.

tool_observations.content:
1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "180:     end

182:     ariane_axi::req_t    dm_axi_m_req,  dm_axi_s_req;
183:     ariane_axi::resp_t   dm_axi_m_resp, dm_axi_s_resp;
184: 
185:     // debug module
186:     dm_top #(
187:         // current implementation only supports 1 hart
188:         .NrHarts              ( 1                         ),
189:         .AxiIdWidth           ( AXI_ID_WIDTH_SLAVES       ),
190:         .AxiAddrWidth         ( AXI_ADDRESS_WIDTH         ),
191:         .AxiDataWidth         ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH            ),
192:         .AxiUserWidth         ( AXI_USER_WIDTH            )
193:     ) i_dm_top (
194: 
195:         .clk_i                ( clk_i                ),
196:         .rst_ni               ( rst_ni               ), // PoR
197:         .testmode_i           ( test_en              ),
198:         .ndmreset_o           ( ndmreset             ),
199:         .dmactive_o           (                      ), // active debug session
200:         .debug_req_o          ( debug_req_core       ),
201:         .unavailable_i        ( '0                   ),
202:         .axi_s_req_i          ( dm_axi_s_req         ),
203:         .axi_s_resp_o         ( dm_axi_s_resp        ),
204:         .axi_m_req_o          ( dm_axi_m_req         ),
205:         .axi_m_resp_i         ( dm_axi_m_resp        ),
206:         .dmi_rst_ni           ( rst_ni               ),
207:         .dmi_req_valid_i      ( debug_req_valid      ),

409: module connectivity_mapping #(
410:     parameter NB_MANAGER      = 4,
411:     parameter NB_SUBORDINATE       = 4,
412:     parameter NB_PRIV_LVL    = 4,   
413:     parameter PRIV_LVL_WIDTH = 4   
414:   )(
415:     input logic [PRIV_LVL_WIDTH-1:0] 	       priv_lvl_i,   
416:     input logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0][NB_PRIV_LVL-1:0] access_ctrl_i,  
417:     
418:     output logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0]  connectivity_map_o
419:   ); 
420: 
421:     
422:   genvar i,j ; 
423: 
424:   generate
425:     
426:     for (i=0; i<NB_SUBORDINATE; i++)
427:     begin
428:       	for (j=0; j<NB_MANAGER; j++)
429:       	begin
430: 	  assign connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]) ; 
431:       	end  		    		      		
432:     end
433: 
434:   endgenerate 
435: 
436: endmodule
